libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For tests that exercise com.mchange.v2.naming.ReferenceableUtils.referenceToObject(...) overloads without an explicit allowedFactoryClassNames Set, set the system property com.mchange.v2.naming.objectFactoryWhitelist to the fully qualified test ObjectFactory class name before the call and restore/clear it in a finally block. Prefer a narrow per-test whitelist; alternatively use the overload that accepts an explicit Set of allowed factory class names.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #2097 [Automation] java run fails for com.mchange:mchange-commons-java:0.4.0; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Using the old 0.2.15-style ReferenceableUtils test unchanged fails on 0.4.0 with NamingException: No ObjectFactory whitelist found.

The artifact declares optional integrations for Typesafe Config and logging frameworks, but they are not required for core meaningful coverage unless tests deliberately target those optional adapters.
Avoid broad or leaked global system-property state; restore the previous property value after each test.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker service is needed, but mchange-commons-java 0.4.0 requires explicit JNDI ObjectFactory whitelisting for ReferenceableUtils coverage.
